concept.name: Supply Fan Status
concept.description: Binary status indicating if supply fan is running

notes: Typically from current sensing relay or differential pressure switch
Used for fan failure alarms and interlock logic
Should match command within reasonable delay
Critical for safety interlocks

concept.name: Discharge Air High Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Discharge Air High Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Discharge Air Low Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Discharge Air Low Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Discharge Air Pressure
concept.description: Static pressure in supply air duct, used to control fan speed

notes: Typically measured 2/3 down the longest duct run
Used as feedback for supply fan VFD speed control
Setpoint typically 1.0-2.0 in.wc for VAV systems
Higher setpoint needed for longer duct runs

concept.name: Return Air High Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Return Air High Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Return Air Low Pressure Alarm
concept.description: Return Air Low Pressure Alarm point

concept.name: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Status
concept.description: Secondary Chilled Water Pump Status point

concept.name: Chilled Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Temperature of chilled water leaving the chiller plant

notes: Typical setpoint 42-44°F for comfort cooling
Higher setpoints (50-55°F) for energy optimization
Must stay above 40°F to prevent coil freezing
Delta T from return typically 10-14°F

concept.name: Chiller Entering Temperature
concept.description: Chiller entering water temperature (return water temperature entering chiller)

concept.name: Chiller Leaving Temperature
concept.description: Chiller leaving water temperature (supply water temperature leaving chiller)

concept.name: Discharge Air Temperature
concept.description: Temperature of air leaving the air handling unit or terminal unit

notes: Cooling mode typically 50-65°F
Heating mode typically 70-110°F
Sometimes called Supply Air Temperature (SAT) downstream of unit
Critical for discharge air temperature control loops

concept.name: Domestic Hot Water Return Temperature
concept.description: Domestic Hot Water Return Temperature point
concept.haystack: domestic hot water entering temp sensor

concept.name: Domestic Hot Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Domestic Hot Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: domestic hot water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Effective Discharge Air Temperature Setpoint
concept.description: Effective Discharge Air Temperature Setpoint point

concept.name: Outdoor Air Temperature
concept.description: Temperature of outside ambient air, used for economizer and lockout control

notes: Critical for economizer enable/disable decisions
Used for heating/cooling lockout control
Often shared across multiple AHUs via BACnet
May be provided by weather service in some systems

concept.name: Primary Chilled Water Return Temperature
concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Return Temperature point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water entering temp sensor

concept.description: Primary Chilled Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: primaryLoop chilled water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Secondary Hot Water Supply Temperature
concept.description: Secondary Hot Water Supply Temperature point
concept.haystack: secondaryLoop hot water leaving temp sensor

concept.name: Zone Temperature
concept.description: Sensed temperature of air in occupied zone or space

notes: Often abbreviated as ZAT (Zone Air Temp) or RAT (Room Air Temp)
In VRF systems, may be called Indoor Unit Temp or FCU Temp
Wireless sensors often use vendor-specific names like EnOcean_Temp_1
When multiple sensors in zone, often suffixed ZoneTemp_1, ZoneTemp_Avg

concept.name: Bypass Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from bypass valve

concept.name: Cooling Tower Isolation Valve
concept.description: Cooling tower isolation valve position or command

concept.name: Cooling Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from cooling valve

concept.name: Heating Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from heating valve

concept.name: Isolation Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from isolation valve

concept.name: Mixing Valve Feedback
concept.description: Actual position/state feedback from mixing valve

equipment.name: Air Handling Unit
equipment.description: Central air conditioning unit that conditions and circulates air through ductwork

equipment.name: Rooftop Unit
equipment.description: Self-contained HVAC unit installed on building roof, combines heating, cooling, and ventilation

equipment.name: Makeup Air Unit
equipment.description: Unit that conditions and supplies fresh outdoor air to replace exhausted air

equipment.name: Dedicated Outdoor Air System
equipment.description: System that conditions 100% outdoor air for ventilation, separate from space conditioning

equipment.name: Fan Coil Unit
equipment.description: Small terminal unit with fan and coil for local heating/cooling, typically without ductwork

equipment.name: Computer Room Air Conditioner
equipment.description: Precision cooling unit designed for data centers and computer rooms with tight temperature control

equipment.name: Computer Room Air Handler
equipment.description: Precision air handler for data centers using chilled water instead of DX cooling

equipment.name: Unit Ventilator
equipment.description: Wall-mounted unit providing ventilation and heating/cooling, common in schools and offices

equipment.name: Packaged Terminal Air Conditioner
equipment.description: Through-wall self-contained unit for heating and cooling individual rooms, common in hotels

equipment.name: Packaged Terminal Heat Pump
equipment.description: Through-wall heat pump unit providing both heating and cooling for individual rooms

equipment.name: Water Source Heat Pump
equipment.description: Heat pump that exchanges heat with a water loop, often in multi-zone buildings

equipment.name: Air Source Heat Pump
equipment.description: Heat pump that exchanges heat with outdoor air for heating and cooling

equipment.name: Energy Recovery Ventilator
equipment.description: Ventilation unit that transfers both heat and moisture between exhaust and supply air

equipment.name: Heat Recovery Ventilator
equipment.description: Ventilation unit that transfers sensible heat only between exhaust and supply air

equipment.name: Air Turnover Unit
equipment.description: High-volume unit for warehouses and large spaces that destratifies air and provides heating

equipment.name: Chiller
equipment.description: Equipment that removes heat from liquid via refrigeration cycle to produce chilled water

equipment.name: Boiler
equipment.description: Equipment that heats water or produces steam using gas, oil, or electric heat

equipment.name: Cooling Tower
equipment.description: Heat rejection device that cools water by evaporation for condenser water systems

equipment.name: Heat Exchanger
equipment.description: Device that transfers heat between two fluids without mixing them

equipment.name: Pump
equipment.description: Equipment that moves water through hydronic heating or cooling systems

equipment.name: Variable Frequency Drive
equipment.description: Electronic device that controls motor speed by varying frequency and voltage

equipment.name: Expansion Tank
equipment.description: Tank that absorbs expansion of heated water and maintains system pressure

equipment.name: Air Separator
equipment.description: Device that removes entrained air from hydronic systems to prevent air locks

equipment.name: Steam-to-Hot-Water Converter
equipment.description: Heat exchanger that converts steam heat to hot water for building heating systems

equipment.name: Electric Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures electrical energy consumption in kWh or demand in kW

equipment.name: Natural Gas Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures natural gas consumption, typically in cubic feet or therms

equipment.name: Water Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures water flow or consumption in gallons or cubic feet

equipment.name: Steam Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures steam flow, typically in pounds per hour

equipment.name: BTU Meter
equipment.description: Device that measures thermal energy by calculating flow rate and temperature differential

equipment.name: fan-motor
equipment.description: fan motor

equipment.name: Variable Air Volume Box
equipment.description: Terminal unit that varies airflow to control zone temperature, used in VAV HVAC systems

equipment.name: Constant Air Volume Box
equipment.description: Terminal unit that maintains constant airflow, temperature controlled by varying supply air temp

equipment.name: Parallel Fan Powered Box
equipment.description: VAV box with parallel fan that draws plenum air, fan runs intermittently during heating

equipment.name: Series Fan Powered Box
equipment.description: VAV box with series fan that runs continuously, mixing primary and plenum air

equipment.name: Chilled Beam
equipment.description: Ceiling-mounted unit using chilled water coils for cooling via convection and radiation

equipment.name: Radiant Panel
equipment.description: Panel system using radiant heat transfer for heating or cooling, ceiling or floor mounted

equipment.name: Baseboard Heater
equipment.description: Wall-mounted heating element at floor level, using hot water or electric resistance

equipment.name: Unit Heater
equipment.description: Self-contained heating unit with fan, using gas, hot water, or electric heat

equipment.name: Induction Unit
equipment.description: Terminal unit that uses high-velocity primary air to induce room air across a coil

equipment.name: Blower Coil Unit
equipment.description: Horizontal or vertical unit with blower and coil, similar to FCU but typically ductless

equipment.name: VRF Outdoor Unit
equipment.description: Outdoor condensing unit for VRF system, connects to multiple indoor units via refrigerant piping

equipment.name: VRF Indoor Unit
equipment.description: Indoor evaporator unit in VRF system, available in various configurations for zone cooling/heating

equipment.name: VRF Branch Selector Box
equipment.description: Distribution box that directs refrigerant flow to individual indoor units in heat recovery VRF systems

common: variable refrigerant flow branch selector box
